code:
export const blogData = [{
  _id: "1",
  slug: "scaling-saas-architecture",
  title: "How to Scale SaaS Architecture for Global Delivery",
  content: `Scaling a SaaS platform requires more than just adding more servers. It involves a strategic shift in how you handle multi-tenancy, data partitioning, and global caching. 

At Kognivex, we recommend a microservices-based approach where each core service can scale independently based on demand. For global delivery, leveraging edge computing and CDNs is critical to reducing latency for users in different geographical regions.

Key considerations for scaling:
1. Database sharding for high-volume data.
2. Implementing robust rate limiting to prevent abuse.
3. Using message queues like RabbitMQ for asynchronous tasks.

By focusing on these architectural pillars, you can ensure your SaaS platform remains responsive even as your user base grows ten-fold.`,
  category: "Engineering",
  image: "https://images.unsplash.com/photo-1451187580459-43490279c0fa?auto=format&fit=crop&q=80&w=800",
  createdAt: "2026-04-15T10:00:00Z"
}, {
  _id: "2",
  slug: "ui-ux-trends-2026",
  title: "UI/UX Trends to Watch in 2026: The Rise of SaaS",
  content: `The digital landscape is evolving rapidly, and in 2026, the focus has shifted towards "invisible UI" and AI-driven personalization. 

Users no longer want complex dashboards; they want systems that anticipate their needs and present the right information at the right time. Dark mode has become the standard, but we're seeing a return to sophisticated, subtle light themes that prioritize readability and soft aesthetics.

What defines modern SaaS UI:
1. Glassmorphism for depth and focus.
2. Micro-interactions that provide instant feedback.
3. Data visualization that tells a story, not just shows numbers.

Designing for 2026 means building for speed, accessibility, and human-centered interaction.`,
  category: "Design",
  image: "https://images.unsplash.com/photo-1558655146-d09347e92766?auto=format&fit=crop&q=80&w=800",
  createdAt: "2026-04-20T14:30:00Z"
}, {
  _id: "3",
  slug: "securing-enterprise-apps",
  title: "Securing Enterprise Applications: A Zero-Trust Approach",
  content: `In an era of sophisticated cyber threats, traditional perimeter security is no longer sufficient. Enterprise applications must adopt a Zero-Trust architecture—never trust, always verify.

This means implementing strict identity verification, least-privileged access, and end-to-end encryption for all data in transit and at rest. At Kognivex, we bake security into the CI/CD pipeline, ensuring every deployment is audited for vulnerabilities before it hits production.

Core Zero-Trust principles:
1. Multi-factor authentication (MFA) for every user.
2. Real-time threat detection and automated response.
3. Micro-segmentation of internal networks.

Security is not a feature; it's the foundation of modern enterprise trust.`,
  category: "Security",
  image: "https://images.unsplash.com/photo-1550751827-4bd374c3f58b?auto=format&fit=crop&q=80&w=800",
  createdAt: "2026-04-25T09:15:00Z"
}];

sourcesContent:
export const blogData = [
  {
    _id: "1",
    slug: "scaling-saas-architecture",
    title: "How to Scale SaaS Architecture for Global Delivery",
    content: `Scaling a SaaS platform requires more than just adding more servers. It involves a strategic shift in how you handle multi-tenancy, data partitioning, and global caching. 

At Kognivex, we recommend a microservices-based approach where each core service can scale independently based on demand. For global delivery, leveraging edge computing and CDNs is critical to reducing latency for users in different geographical regions.

Key considerations for scaling:
1. Database sharding for high-volume data.
2. Implementing robust rate limiting to prevent abuse.
3. Using message queues like RabbitMQ for asynchronous tasks.

By focusing on these architectural pillars, you can ensure your SaaS platform remains responsive even as your user base grows ten-fold.`,
    category: "Engineering",
    image: "https://images.unsplash.com/photo-1451187580459-43490279c0fa?auto=format&fit=crop&q=80&w=800",
    createdAt: "2026-04-15T10:00:00Z"
  },
  {
    _id: "2",
    slug: "ui-ux-trends-2026",
    title: "UI/UX Trends to Watch in 2026: The Rise of SaaS",
    content: `The digital landscape is evolving rapidly, and in 2026, the focus has shifted towards "invisible UI" and AI-driven personalization. 

Users no longer want complex dashboards; they want systems that anticipate their needs and present the right information at the right time. Dark mode has become the standard, but we're seeing a return to sophisticated, subtle light themes that prioritize readability and soft aesthetics.

What defines modern SaaS UI:
1. Glassmorphism for depth and focus.
2. Micro-interactions that provide instant feedback.
3. Data visualization that tells a story, not just shows numbers.

Designing for 2026 means building for speed, accessibility, and human-centered interaction.`,
    category: "Design",
    image: "https://images.unsplash.com/photo-1558655146-d09347e92766?auto=format&fit=crop&q=80&w=800",
    createdAt: "2026-04-20T14:30:00Z"
  },
  {
    _id: "3",
    slug: "securing-enterprise-apps",
    title: "Securing Enterprise Applications: A Zero-Trust Approach",
    content: `In an era of sophisticated cyber threats, traditional perimeter security is no longer sufficient. Enterprise applications must adopt a Zero-Trust architecture—never trust, always verify.

This means implementing strict identity verification, least-privileged access, and end-to-end encryption for all data in transit and at rest. At Kognivex, we bake security into the CI/CD pipeline, ensuring every deployment is audited for vulnerabilities before it hits production.

Core Zero-Trust principles:
1. Multi-factor authentication (MFA) for every user.
2. Real-time threat detection and automated response.
3. Micro-segmentation of internal networks.

Security is not a feature; it's the foundation of modern enterprise trust.`,
    category: "Security",
    image: "https://images.unsplash.com/photo-1550751827-4bd374c3f58b?auto=format&fit=crop&q=80&w=800",
    createdAt: "2026-04-25T09:15:00Z"
  }
];
